tools.name: scaffold_managed_skill
tools.description: Create or update a managed skill in {workspaceDir}/skills. The skill becomes available for skill_load when a valid top-level SKILL.md is written under the skill directory. Never persist a skill without explicit user consent. Before persisting, test the snippet: write to a temp file with bash and run with `bun run /tmp/vellum-eval/snippet.ts`. Iterate up to 3 attempts, then ask the user. Clean up temp files after. Do not use file_write for temp files outside the working directory. After a skill is written, the next turn may run in a recreated conversation due to file-watcher eviction - continue normally.

skill_id.description: Unique identifier for the skill (lowercase slug, e.g. "my-skill").

description.description: Short description of what the skill does.

body_markdown.description: The full skill body in markdown - instructions, prompts, templates, etc.

emoji.description: Optional emoji icon for the skill.

category.description: Optional single lowercase category for grouping the skill in the Skills UI sidebar. Must be one of the published categories — a value outside this list shows under All with no sidebar bucket, so always pick the closest fit: browsing, calendar, commerce, content, development, email, health, integrations, messaging, productivity, system, voice.

overwrite.description: Whether to overwrite an existing skill with the same ID (default: false).

includes.description: Optional list of child skill IDs that this skill includes (metadata only, no auto-activation).

activation_hints.description: Optional trigger phrases describing the situations where this skill should activate, phrased as the observed intent (e.g. "user asks to deploy staging"). Surfaced in memory as a "Use when: …" clause so the skill is retrievable by intent, not just by name.

avoid_when.description: Optional situations where this skill should NOT be used. Surfaced in memory as an "Avoid when: …" clause to steer retrieval away from the wrong contexts.

path.description: Path relative to the skill directory (e.g. "references/failure-modes.md"). Absolute paths and ".." segments are rejected.

files.description: Optional companion files written under the skill directory (e.g. references/*.md referenced from SKILL.md). Paths must resolve inside the skill folder.

add_to_index.description: Deprecated no-op compatibility field. Skills are discovered from top-level SKILL.md files.

tools.name: delete_managed_skill
tools.description: Delete a managed skill directory from {workspaceDir}/skills. Never delete a skill without explicit user confirmation. After deletion, the next turn may run in a recreated conversation due to file-watcher eviction - continue normally.

skill_id.description: The ID of the managed skill to delete.

remove_from_index.description: Deprecated no-op compatibility field. Skill deletion does not edit SKILLS.md.

tools.name: find_similar_skills
tools.description: Find the existing skills most similar to a goal. Scores the goal against the skill catalog's capability pages and returns a ranked shortlist of nearest skills, each with its name, description, source (bundled, managed, plugin, workspace, or extra), and similarity score. For a managed hit it also returns `author` — "assistant" if the assistant authored that skill (so it may be overwritten) or "user" if a person did (off-limits); `author` is omitted for non-managed sources and for managed skills with no recorded author. Read-only — it never creates, edits, or deletes anything.

goal.description: The goal or intent to match against existing skills.

limit.description: Maximum number of nearest skills to return (default: 5).
